title: Register Symptoms
description: The system shall allow a patient to register their symptoms.
rationale: To ensure that the system can handle and process patient case information effectively, it is necessary for patients to be able to report their symptoms.
business_value: Improves the efficiency of case management by enabling timely symptom recording, which aids in faster diagnosis and treatment planning.

source.description: As a patient, I need to register my symptoms so that the system can handle my case.

acceptance_criteria: The system records my symptom information.
preconditions: The user is logged in to the system.
postconditions: The symptom information is recorded and stored in the system database.
A confirmation message is displayed to the patient indicating that their symptoms have been successfully registered.
functional_constraints: Symptom information must be recorded accurately and securely.
security_requirements: Symptom data must be encrypted during transit and at rest to protect patient privacy.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within 5 seconds for symptom registration.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1,000 concurrent patient registrations per minute.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly inform patients how their symptoms were not recorded and suggest next steps.

edge_cases: Patient inputs symptoms in multiple languages.
System fails to record symptom information due to network issues.
dependencies: User authentication system for patient login.
Database system to store symptom information securely.

interfaces.name: Symptom Registration API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Symptom Registration API to record and store patient symptoms in the database.

interfaces.name: Patient Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Patient Database to persistently store symptom information.

test_cases.description: Verify that a patient can register their symptoms using valid input.
test_cases.expected_result: Symptom information is recorded and stored successfully in the database with a confirmation message displayed to the patient.

test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles network failures gracefully when recording symptom information.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message to the patient and retry the registration upon successful network reconnection.
